q: Which of the following best describes the process of water movement through xylem vessels from roots to leaves?
options: Active transport of water molecules up the xylem using ATP
Root pressure alone forces water all the way to the leaf canopy
Osmosis at the roots combined with gravity pulling water upward
Transpiration pull creates tension that draws water up via cohesion-tension through continuous water columns
explanation: Evaporation at leaf stomata creates negative pressure (tension); water's cohesion and adhesion allow it to be pulled up continuously from root to leaf.
